mapping.a_element: conformal factor multiplying the Lorentz-Minkowski metric to reproduce the Robertson-Walker metric
mapping.b_element: warp factor multiplying the 4D Poincare-invariant (Minkowski) metric along the extra dimension

mapping.a_element: flat Lorentz-Minkowski base metric that the RW metric is made conformal to
mapping.b_element: 4D Minkowski slice on which the warped 5D braneworld metric is built

brief_justification: The only real overlap is the superficial one that both write a scalar factor times a Minkowski metric, but the machinery differs decisively: paper A is a purely kinematic differential-geometry classification, deriving all coordinate transformations and conformal factors that recast the 4D Robertson-Walker metric into forms conformal to flat spacetime, with no field equations or dynamics. Paper B instead solves 5D Weyl-integrable (non-Riemannian Kaluza-Klein) field equations to obtain a dynamical braneworld solution whose warp factor localizes the graviton and resolves the mass hierarchy a la Randall-Sundrum, so there is no shared governing process to map role-for-role.
